threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look up when the Tube's rumbling beneath - locals just brace for the shake and carry on like it's the weather.
Caught a night bus home last week; a guy was offering 'emergency haircuts' with scissors from his backpack. Brixton never sleeps, huh?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I heard the guy at the corner kebab stand shout 'Zwei Minuten!' as if it were a friendly warning, not a threat. That's Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you grab a döner at 2 AM after a night out, while cats casually roam the late-night schwarzes Straßenfest.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they roll their eyes hearing about another BART delay, yet still insist on taking it because parking's worse.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: The other day, I saw a pigeon hitching a ride on the back of a Muni bus, just chilling. Only in this city do the birds have the same commute as us.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists always assume Gràcia is just about Gaudí. But the true vibe is hidden in the little plazas where locals sip vermut, not in La Pedrera.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still fumbling with the metro tickets, looking around like it’s a maze. Locals know the shortcuts by now.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it Dalston Junction instead of just 'The Junction'—that'll give you away every time.
Caught a guy serenading the bus driver with an old Whitney song last night. Bus was packed, but everyone was in good spirits, singing along.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I realized I was home? Hearing a spontaneous jam session from the courtyard while waiting for the bus. It’s the little things.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do they have late-night Späti cocktail menus. Grab a mojito in your pajamas while waiting for the tram home after 2 AM.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they have a favorite taco truck and can name three different BART delays just from memory.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the Muni stuck at Civic Center yesterday because of a dog on the tracks—classic SF. Only here does your commute pause for a pup.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists flock to Park Güell, but miss the hidden gems like Plaça de la Vila, where locals actually gather for community events.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they still search for the ‘o’ in ‘carrer’ on their map instead of just following the locals. It's the little things.
